Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322 5660          322 5660              322 5901           322 6441            322 6444


June 25, 1981


Gordon C. Phillips
City Attorney
City of Redondo Beach
415 Diamond Street
Redondo Beach, CA 90277

Dear Mr. Phillips:

Your letter dated May 20, 1981, was received by the Commission
on June 4, 1981. We appreciate your request for written advice
with regard to the Harbor Review Board. However, our regulations,
2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(b)(1) require that prior to providing
written advice:

"The identity of the person whose duties are in question
shall be provided with the request for written advice".

Consequently, we must ask that you provide us with the name
of the individual on whose behalf you have requested this advice.
In addition to seeking our advice, you may wish to request the
Attorney General's advice relative to the duties and obligations
imposed by Government Code §1090 et seq. Since it is not a part
of the Political Reform Act, then we are not authorized to render
advice in relation to its provisions.

As soon as we have received this information we will endeavor
to promptly respond to your request.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Ledigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:gs

GORDON C. PHILLIPS
CITY ATTORNEY

TELEPHONE
(213) 372-1171

FPPC
Jun 4 8 41 AM '81

CITY OF REDONDO BEACH
CALIFORNIA
415 DIAMOND STREET
REDONDO BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90277
May 20, 1981

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

QUESTION NO. 1

Does a Harbor Lessee have a conflict of interest per se when
sitting as a member of the city's Harbor Review Board?

QUESTION NO. 2

If the answer to Question No.1 is "no," does a Harbor Lessee
have a conflict of interest in sitting on the Harbor Review Board
when making a determination regarding new development within the
harbor which has a financial effect on such lessee?

FACTUAL BACKGROUND

The Harbor Review Board is a creature of contract, not ordinance.
A copy of the lease provision creating the Harbor Review Board
and the operational guidelines are enclosed for your examination.

The question arises as to whether or not a Harbor Lessee has a
conflict of interest per se under Article I, Section 2(1), (2),
and if not, whether such conflict exists as to new development
which would have a financial effect on the official's financial
interest.

By way of example: The present lessee on the Harbor Review Board
maintains and operates a hotel, restaurant, and marina within the
harbor. Recent proposals to construct a hotel and parking
structure came before the Harbor Review Board. The hotel and

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 20, 1981
Page 2

parking structure are in close proximity to the lessee's hotel
and marina, and which would effect that lessee's financial
interest.

While the Harbor Review Board is designed to render advisory
opinions to the council, from time to time, its conclusions have
been treated as final on matters considered to be minor in nature.

Very truly yours,

Gordon C. Phillips
City Attorney

Encl.

ment District is to encourage and provide a means for effectuating development of a more desirable environment by the application of site planning techniques not permitted through the literal application of the zoning and subdivision regulations.
(§ 2, Ord. 1846 c.s., eff. September 9, 1964)

Sec. 10-2.1311. Uses permitted.

A. The following uses shall be permitted in the P.D Planned Development District:

1. P.D: All uses permitted in any district, as established by this chapter;

2. P.D-R: All uses permitted in any residential district, as established by this chapter;

3. P.D-C: All uses permitted in any commercial district, as established by this chapter; and

4. P.D-I: All uses permitted in any industrial district, as established by this chapter.

B. Such uses shall be submitted for review and recommendation to the appropriate board or commission as set forth herein and shall be subject to public hearing pursuant to the provisions of Section 10-2.1701 of Article 6 of this chapter.

C. The uses approved shall assure that the degree of compatibility made one of the purposes of this chapter, shall be maintained with respect to the proposed use on a particular site and location, giving consideration to other existing and potential uses within the general area in which such use is located or proposed to be located.
(§ 2, Ord. 1846 c.s., eff. September 9, 1964)

Sec. 10-2.1312. Procedures.

All applications and plans for the use and development of land in these districts shall be submitted to the Planning Commission for review and recommendation except as follows:

1. The Harbor Area, including uses, improvements, development, and parking, shall be submitted to the Harbor Review Board. The legal description of the Harbor Area is as follows: Beginning at the intersection of the northerly boundary line of the City and the center line of Harbor Drive (70 feet wide); thence southerly along said center line of Harbor Drive to its intersection with the easterly prolongation of the southerly line of 11th Street; thence westerly along the said easterly prolongation of the southerly line of 11th Street and the southerly line of 11th Street to its intersection with the easterly line of the "Strand" (30 feet wide); thence southerly along the

Way; thence easterly along the said westerly prolongation of the

2. The area in this district bounded by Francisca, Elena,
Vanderbilt, and Reef Streets shall be referred to the Civic Center
Architectural Review Board prior to submission to the Planning Com-
mission.

3. Parks, landscaping, and recreation areas in this district
shall be referred to the Parks and Recreation Department for study
and recommendation prior to submission to the Planning Commission.
The application shall be accompanied by a site plan as specified by
Section 10-2.1609 of Article 5 of this chapter (site plan review).
(§ 2, Ord. 1846 ex., eff. September 9, 1964)

Sec. 10-2.1313. Development standards.

A. In order to accomplish the objectives of this section, it may
be desirable or essential to vary the provisions of the subdivision regu-
lations of the City; provided, however, proper standards shall be
equally applied where necessary as a condition of approval.

B. The location of buildings, yards, density, height of build-
ings, and other improvement requirements shall be specified for each
separate use of land within the P-D Districts and in accordance with
approved development plans.

C. The applicant may be required, as a condition of approval,
to dedicate land for street or park purposes where indicated on the
General Plan and to restrict areas perpetually as open space for com-
mon use by appropriate covenants.

D. The site for the proposed use shall be of adequate size and
shape to accommodate the proposed use, yards, courts, walls, fences,
and landscaping, buffers, parking, and other required features.

E. Adequate street access shall be provided to carry the quan-
tity and kind of traffic generated by the proposed use and designed
to provide adequate ingress and egress for fire-fighting equipment.

HARBOR DEVELOPMENT COMMITTEE
APPOINTMENT OF MEMBERS

Mayor Hayward appointed the following persons to the Harbor
Development Committee for the terms as listed:

Councilman Ebben, to serve until June 30, 1981.
Councilman Nielsen, to serve until June 30, 1979.
Randy Kimose, to serve until June 30, 1981.
H. Lyle Smith, to serve until June 30, 1979.
David Blausey, to serve until June 30, 1981.
Catherine Gielicz, to serve until June 30, 1981.
Mary Davis, to serve until June 30, 1978.

It was moved by Councilman Nielsen and seconded by
Councilman McSweeney to concur in the Mayor's appointments as
listed to the Harbor Development Committee. The motion carried
by the following vote:

AYES:         Goddard, Ebben, McSweeney,
              Mitchell, Nielsen;

NOES:         None.

LEASE AMENDMENTS WITH
HARBOR MASTER LESSEES
AND RESTRUCTURING OF
HARBOR REVIEW BOARD

The City Manager presented a synopsis dated July 1, 1977,
transmitting Harbor Review Board Operational Guidelines for the
restructuring of the Harbor Review Board to consolidate the
existing functions of the Board and the Leasing Committee in
accordance with instructions of the City Council. He recommended

the City Council approve the Operational Guidelines for the
Harbor Review Board as presented.

The City Manager stated that 10 of 14 existing Harbor master
leases were flexible enough to permit the proposed consolidation,
but it had been necessary to negotiate lease amendments with the
remaining four master lessees to assure compatibility with the
proposed structure of the Board. He presented and recommended
approval of the following Lease Amendments between the City of
Redondo Beach and the Harbor master lessees as listed, and
stated the Amendments had been approved as to form by the City
Attorney:

Amendment No. 8 to the Lease with Pier Properties,
Limited (Hal Pollack).

Amendment No. 12 to the Lease with Robert D. Moudy
(Port Royal Marina).

Amendment No. 14 to the Lease with Charles G. Johnston,
Incorporated (Redondo Beach Marina).

Amendment No. 6 to the Lease between the City and
Portefino, Incorporated (Mary Davis).

Mayor Hayward stated he expected to be able to propose
nominations to the Harbor Review Board at the next City Council
meeting.

Councilman McSweeney stated he felt that the members of
the City Council not appointed to the Harbor Review Board
should have the opportunity to propose the names of three of
the public members to be nominated to the Board.

It was moved by Councilman Nielsen and seconded by
Councilman Ebben that the Operational Guidelines for the Harbor
Review Board as presented be approved and adopted; further, that
the lease amendments as listed be approved, that the Mayor be
authorized to sign same on behalf of the City, and the City Clerk
attest to the Mayor's signature. The motion carried by the
following vote:

AYES:         Goddard, Ebben, McSweeney,
               Mitchell, Nielsen;

NOES:         None.

                                                jl 7-11-77

LEASE AMENDMENTS WITH
HARBOR MASTER LESSEES
AND OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES
FOR HARBOR REVIEW BOARD

The City Manager presented a synopsis dated July 1, 1977,
transmitting Harbor Review Board Operational Guidelines for the
restructuring of the Harbor Review Board to consolidate the
existing functions of the Board and the Leasing Committee in
accordance with instructions of the City Council. He also
presented a memorandum dated July 5, 1977, setting forth a
correction under Section 2 of Article 1 of the Guidelines. He
recommended the City Council approve the Operational Guidelines
for the Harbor Review Board as amended by the memorandum of
July 5, 1977.

The City Manager stated that 10 of 14 existing Harbor master
leases were flexible enough to permit the proposed consolidation,
but it had been necessary to negotiate lease amendments with the
remaining four master lessees to assure compatibility with the
proposed structure of the Board. He presented and recommended
approval of the following Lease Amendments between the City of
Redondo Beach and the Harbor master lessees, and stated the
Amendments had been approved as to form by the City Attorney:

Amendment No. 8 to the Lease with Pier Properties,
Limited (Hal Pollack).

Amendment No. 12 to the Lease with Robert D. Moudy
(Port Royal Marina).

Amendment No. 14 to the Lease with Charles G. Johnston,
Incorporated (Redondo Beach Marina).

Amendment No. 6 to the Lease between the City and
Portofino, incorporated (Mary Davis).

It was moved by Councilman Mitchell and seconded by
Councilman Nielsen that consideration of the Operational
Guidelines for the Harbor Review Board and the lease amendments
as listed be tabled. The motion carried by the following vote:

AYES:        Goddard, McSweeney,
             Mitchell, Nielsen;

NOES:        None;

ABSENT:      Ebben.                                    j1 7-5-77


X-filed

-11-

AGENDA ITEM 24

SYNOPSIS

TO: Mayor & City Council

FROM: Robert Riley, City Manager          Date: 1 July 1977

SUBJECT: Restructuring of the Harbor Review Board


SUMMARY:

In accordance with City Council instructions, efforts to consolidate the
existing functions of the Harbor Review Board and the Leasing Committee
under a revamped Harbor Review Board have been pursued. One of the
necessary steps in this effort was to negotiate lease amendments with
four (4) of the fourteen (14) harbor area lessees. Ten (10) of the
City's existing leases in the harbor area are flexible enough to permit
the consolidation and restructuring of the Review Board, but unfortunately,
four (4) of the older leases (Redondo Beach Marina, Portofino, Port Royal
and Pier Properties) required amending in order to assure compatibility
with the proposed structure of the Board.

Therefore, identical lease Amendments have been negotiated with the
four (4) affected lessees, approved as to form by the City Attorney, and
are now ready for City Council consideration. The Amendments basically
provide that the Review Board shall consist of seven (7) persons selected
by the Mayor, subject to the approval of the City Council, from among the
following:

a) Two members of the City Council;

b) Four public members having knowledge or expertise in the following areas:

   o recreational harbors and/or commercial real estate;
   o architecture and/or planning;
   o business and/or finance;
   o building and/or engineering

c) one member from among those individuals or entities within
   the Harbor and Pier areas having a lease or sublease with the
   City.

Additionally, considerable attention has been given to the development
of operational guidelines (copy attached) for the restructured Harbor
Review Board. The proposed guidelines delineate the advisory nature
of the Board's relationship to the City Council and detail the leasing
and developmental matters subject to the Board's purview. The guidelines
also provide that members of the Board shall serve staggered
four year terms and that the lessees members shall serve a one year term.

PRESENTED TO THE
CITY COUNCIL
JUL 1 1 1977      JUL 5 1977

RECOMMENDATION:

That the City Council consolidate the existing functions of the Harbor
Review Board and the Leasing Committee under a restructured Harbor Review
Board and in furtherance of that action:

1) approve the proposed operational guidelines for the Harbor
Review Board; and,

2) approve the following lease amendments and authorize the Mayor
and City Clerk to sign same in behalf of the City:

o Amendment No. 8 to the Lease between the City and
Pier Properties, Ltd. (Hai Pollack)

o Amendment No. 12 to the Lease between the City and Robert
D. Moudy (Port Royal)

o Amendment No. 14 to the Lease between the City and Charles
G. Johnston, Inc. (Redondo Beach Marina)

o Amendment No. 6 to the Lease between the City and Portofino,
Inc. (Mary Davis).

Robert L. Riley, City Manager


By ___________________________
Richard W. Parsons
Harbor Director

DEPARTMENTS INVOLVED:

Harbor

INTER-OFFICE MEMORANDUM        City of Redondo Beach

TO:     Robert L. Riley, City Manager                                    IMP 77156
FROM:   Richard Parsons, Harbor Director                    DATE: 9 July 1977

SUBJECT: Harbor Review Board Operational Guidelines


A review of the proposed operational guidelines for restructured
Harbor Review Board has revealed that an obsolete phase was in-
advertently included in a portion of Section 2 of Article 1 of
said guidelines. Section 2 should read as follows: (the revised
phase is indicated in sub-section 1 by double underlining):

Section 2. - Objectives and Powers. The primary objective of the
Board is to provide effective and efficient supervision and
management of the harbor leasing and developmental activities
so as to: (i) maximize the economic benefits inuring to the
City by way of the City's contractual and lease arrangements
with its harbor lessees; (ii) maximize the public use and
patronage of the harbor's recreational facilities and com-
mercial enterprises; (iii) preserve and complement the en-
vironmental and aesthetic qualities of the City's harbor and
harbor facilities; and (iv) promote and accommodate commerce
and navigation in compliance with the Tidelands Grant. In
order to accomplish the foregoing objectives, the Board shall:

(1)  Review and Recommend to the Council on the design and
     __construction of new harbor facilities and related im-
     provements, and any additions to or modifications of
     such facilities or improvements;

(2)  Review and recommend to the Council on the various con-
     tractual and lease arrangements relating to the harbor,
     harbor facilities and related improvements.


                                        Richard W. Parsons
                                        Harbor Director

/b


                                    PRESENTED TO THE
                                    CITY COUNCIL ON
                                    JUL - 9 1977

HARBOR REVIEW BOARD

OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

ARTICLE I: GENERAL

Section 1. - Purposes. These Guidelines are intended to set forth the
operational framework within which the Harbor Review Board ("Board")
and its Staff shall conduct the Board's business and perform its
duties and functions as such matters shall be directed and established
by the City Council of the City of Redondo Beach ("Council"). The
primary responsibility of the Board is to review and recommend to
the City Council on leasing and developmental matters related to the
improvement and operation of King Harbor as described in Section
12-1.108 of Chapter 1, Title 12 of the Redondo Beach
Municipal Code. However, in the performance of these obligations,
it is acknowledged that the Board must, to a very substantial degree,
depend upon its Staff. Accordingly, a significant portion of these
Guidelines is devoted to the specification of matters which are referred as Staff Functions to be handled and dealt with by the Staff
without the prior approval of the Board.

Section 2. - Objectives and Powers. The primary objective of the Board
is to provide effective and efficient supervision and management of
the harbor leasing and developmental activities so as to: (i) maximize the economic benefits accruing to the City by pay of the City's
contractual and lease arrangements with its harbor lessees; (ii)
maximize the public use and patronage of the harbor's recreational
facilities and commercial enterprises; (iii) preserve and promote
the environmental and aesthetic qualities of the City's harbor and
harbor facilities; and (iv) promote and accommodate commerce and
navigation in compliance with the Tidelands Grant. In order to
accomplish the foregoing objectives, the Board shall:

(1) Review and Recommend to the City Council on the design and
construction of new harbor facilities and related improvements, and any additions to or modifications of such
facilities or improvements;

(2) Review and recommend to the Council on the various con-
tractual and lease arrangements relating to the harbor,
harbor facilities and related improvements.

ARTICLE II: Composition of the Board

Section 1 - General. The Harbor Review Board shall consist of seven (7)
persons selected by the Mayor of Redondo Beach subject to the approval
of the City Council of Redondo Beach from among the following:

1.1 Two members of the Council;

1.2 Four public members having knowledge or expertise in
the following areas:

• recreational harbors and/or commercial real estate;
• architecture and/or planning
• business and/or finance
• building and/or engineering

1.3 one member from among those individuals or entities within
the Harbor and Pier areas having a lease or sublease with
the City.

Section 2 - Term of Office.

2.1 Members of the Council shall serve on the Board for periods
that coincide with their term on the Council;

2.2 Public members shall serve four year terms commencing on
July 1st. of any given year. The members first appointed
to the Board shall be appointed in such a manner that each
succeeding July 1st, the term of one of their number
shall expire. Thereafter, any appointment to fill an un-
expired term shall be for such unexpired period.

2.3 The lessee member shall serve a one year term commencing on
July 1st. of each year.

Section 3 - Organization Meeting. The Board shall meet as soon after
the first day of July of every year as practical for the purpose of
organizing. At such meeting, the Board shall organize itself by
electing a Chairman and establishing a regular monthly meeting date.
All proceeding of the Board shall be open to the public.

Section 4 - Vacancies. Vacancies occurring on the Board shall be filled
by appointment by the Mayor subject to confirmation by the Council,
and in the event that any member of the Board shall absent himself
from the regular monthly meeting of the Board for a period of sixty
(60) days consecutively following the last scheduled meeting attended
by such member, unless by permission of the Council expressed in its
official minutes, or in the event such member shall be convicted of
crime involving moral turpitude, his office shall become vacant and
shall be so declared by the Council.

ARTICLE III: CONCURRENT POWERS AND FUNCTIONS
OF BOARD AND ITS STAFF

Section 1. - General. The Board shall serve as the Council's repre-
sentative in reviewing leasing and developmental matters pertaining

-2-

to the harbor and the pier and, and in no doing, the Board shall act
primarily as a recommending body. The Staff will be responsible for
implementing the recommendations of the Board and, to the extent
authorized by these Guidelines or otherwise directed by the Board, or
Council shall be empowered to act and to conduct the Board's business
without first obtaining the consent or approval of the Board.

Section 2. - Powers and Functions Vested in the Board. The following
powers and functions may be exercised by the Board, except in in-
stances when the Council indicates that a different procedure is to
be followed, though such matters will, to a substantial degree, be
dependent upon information and data accumulated and recommendations
made by the Staff:

(1) The Board shall make reports and recommendations to the
Council on all matters relating to leasing and developmental
activities within the harbor upon which the Council must
act or approve;

(2) All matters within the preview of the Board, which must be
submitted to the City Council, shall first be reviewed and/
or approved by the Board prior to reporting to the Council.

Section 3. - Board and Staff Responsibilities Pertaining to Lease and
Sublease Arrangements.

3.1 Proposals to Lease: The Staff shall serve as the initial
contact between the City and potential developers for
sites located in the harbor area. Further, the Staff
shall screen such proposals based upon information provid-
ed to the Staff with respect to the proposed development and the developers and shall select one or more such
proposals to be presented to and considered by the Board.
The Board shall, after reviewing the Staff's report, take
such action as it deems appropriate to arrive at the
recommendation to be made to the Council.

3.2 Pre-Lease and Construction Arrangements: Upon the
approval of the Council and formalization of the pre-lease
and construction arrangement for a proposed project,
the Staff shall be responsible for monitoring the developer
performance and for reviewing, approving and recommending
changes to the conceptual developmental plans, preliminary
plans, working drawings, and specifications which may be
submitted by the developer in accordance with said con-
tractual arrangement. At any stage of the design process,
and only after the Staff is satisfied with the documents
and materials submitted, such plans, drawings and specifi-
cations may be submitted to the Board for its review, ap-
proval and such further actions and recommendations as the
Board may deem appropriate.

-3-

3.3 Lease or Sublease Arrangements: The Staff shall be responsible
for negotiating the terms of the final Lease or Sublease arrangements
between the City and a developer/leasee within limits established
by the Board, subject to the approval of the Council.

3.4 Administration of Leases and Subleases: The Staff shall monitor the
day-to-day operations and activities of the harbor lessees to insure
compliance with the terms of their respective lease arrangements with
the City. It shall also be the responsibility of the Staff to in-
sure and make the necessary arrangements for compliance by the City
with the City's obligations under such lease arrangements. The
Staff shall periodically report to the Board as to the results of
its activities with respect to these matters.

3.5 Modification Leases and Subleases: The Board shall be responsible
for reviewing and approving, and thereafter reporting and recommend-
ing to the Council, all matters which may require a formal modificati
or amendment to a lease between the City and any harbor lessee. The
Board shall not have authority to review and approve subleases be-
tween the City's lessees and their subtenants.

Section 4. - Delegation of Authority by the Board to the Staff.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Board may delegate
to the Staff any or all of its functions pertaining to
the Lease and pre-lease arrangements above described.

ARTICLE IV: SPECIFIC POWERS AND
FUNCTIONS OF STAFF

Section 1. - Operation of Harbor. The Harbor Director and the Staff shall
be responsible for the day to day operation of the Harbor and the
Board.

Section 2. Ministerial and Minor Discretionary Matters. The Staff may,
without Board approval, authorize action and act on matters about which
the Board has previously given the Staff objective criteria or measure-
ments or, on other matters which may involve the exercise of personal
judgment or discretion, so long as such matters do not have a significant
impact upon the harbor or the harbor facilities.

Section 3. - Emergencies. The Staff shall have the power and authority
to take such action as may be necessary or appropriate to prevent or
mitigate loss of, or damage to, life, health, property, or essential
public services in the harbor area which may arise from a sudden un-
expected occurrence, which demands immediate action. This authority to
act without Board approval shall expire upon the passing or subsidence
of such emergency.

Section 4. - Miscellaneous. Without in anyway limiting the foregoing
powers and separate functions of the Board, the Staff may act upon
the following matters without having first obtained Board approval:

1. Authorizing action in the conduct and performance of normal mainte-
nance, repair and minor alterations to existing harbor facilities
by the City or its harbor lessees or their agents;

-4-

2. Authorizing the replacement or reconstruction of harbor
facilities to their prior or original condition so long as
no substantive changes in the use or expansion of such
facilities will result from such replacement or reconstruction;

3. Authorizing the construction and/or installation of small
structures, improvements, facilities or equipment which will
have no significant impact on the harbor, its operation or
other facilities, and are in compliance with currently effective
lease or other contractual arrangements;

4. Authorizing the installation and/or expansion of utility
services and facilities in the harbor area which may be necessary by reason of matters previously approved by the Board
or the Council;

5. Authorizing such interim or temporary actions made necessary
by reason of matters previously approved by the Board or the
Council, or which may arise from an emergency;

6. Authorizing the collection and dissemination of data and information relating to the Board, the harbor or harbor
facilities;

7. Authorizing the conduct of planning and feasibility studies
as may be directed by the Board or the Council;

8. Authorizing the conduct of inspections of the harbor and harbor
facilities pursuant to the duties and functions of the Board,
including the establishment of schedules for the conduct of
such inspections;

9. Authorizing and/or undertaking other tasks or projects which
may be assigned to the Staff by the Board or the Council.

-5-

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444


June 25, 1981


Gordon C. Phillips
City Attorney
City of Redondo Beach
415 Diamond Street
Redondo Beach, CA 90277

Dear Mr. Phillips:

     Your letter dated May 20, 1981, was received by the Commission
on June 4, 1981. We appreciate your request for written advice
with regard to the Harbor Review Board. However, our regulations,
2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(b)(1) require that prior to providing
written advice:

     "The identity of the person whose duties are in question
     shall be provided with the request for written advice".

     Consequently, we must ask that you provide us with the name
of the individual on whose behalf you have requested this advice.
In addition to seeking our advice, you may wish to request the
Attorney General's advice relative to the duties and obligations
imposed by Government Code §1090 et seq. Since it is not a part
of the Political Reform Act, then we are not authorized to render
advice in relation to its provisions.

to promptly respond to your request.

REL:gs

sections.question:
NO. 1

Does a Harbor Lessee have a conflict of interest per se when sitting as a member of the city's Harbor Review Board?

QUESTION NO. 2

If the answer to Question No. 1 is "no," does a Harbor Lessee have conflict of interest in sitting on the Harbor Review Board when making a determination regarding new development within the harbor which has a financial effect on such lessee?
sections.conclusion:
In accordance with City Council instructions, efforts to consolidate the existing functions of the Harbor Review Board and the Leasing Committee under a revamped Harbor Review Board have been pursued. One of the necessary steps in this effort was to negotiate lease amendments with four (4) of the fourteen (14) harbor area lessees. Ten (10) of the City's existing leases in the harbor area are flexible enough to permit the consolidation and restructuring of the Review Board, but unfortunately, four (4) of the older leases (Redondo Beach Marina, Portofino, Port Royal and Pier Properties) required amending in order to assure compatibility with the proposed structure of the Board.

Therefore, identical lease Amendments have been negotiated with the four (4) effected lessees, approved as to form by the City Attorney, and are now ready for City Council consideration. The Amendments basically provide that the Review Board shall consist of seven (7) persons selected by the Mayor, subject to the approval of the City Council, from among the following:

a) Two members of the City Council;

b) Four public members having knowledge or expertise in the following areas:
   o recreational harbors and/or commercial real estate;
   o architecture and/or planning;
   o business and/or finance;
   o building and/or engineering

c) one member from among those individuals or entities within the Harbor and Pier areas having a lease or sublease with the City.

Additionally, considerable attention has been given to the development of operational guidelines (copy attached) for the restructured Harbor Review Board. The proposed guidelines delineate the advisory nature of the Board's relationship to the City Council and detail the leasing and developmental matters subject to the Board's purview. The guidelines also provide that the public members of the board shall serve staggered four year terms and that the lessees members shall serve a one year term.

RECOMMENDATION:

That the City Council consolidate the existing functions of the Harbor Review Board and the Leasing Committee under a restructured Harbor Review Board and in furtherance of that action:

1) approve the proposed operational guidelines for the Harbor Review Board; and,

2) approve the following lease amendments and authorize the Mayor and City Clerk to sign same in behalf of the City:

   o Amendment No. 8 to the Lease between the City and Pier Properties, Ltd. (Hal Pollack)
   o Amendment No. 12 to the Lease between the City and Robert D. Moudy (Port Royal)
   o Amendment No. 14 to the Lease between the City and Charles G. Johnston, Inc. (Redondo Beach Marina)
   o Amendment No. 6 to the Lease between the City and Portofino, Inc. (Mary Davis).

Robert L. Riley, City Manager

By ____________________________
Richard W. Parsons
Harbor Director

DEPARTMENTS INVOLVED:
Harbor

TO: Robert L. Riley, City Manager

FROM: Richard Parsons, Harbor Director

DATE: 5 July 1977

SUBJECT: Harbor Review Board Operational Guidelines

A review of the proposed operational guidelines for restructured Harbor Review Board has revealed that an obsolete phase was inadvertently included in a portion of Section 2 of Article I of said guidelines. Section 2 should read as follows: (the revised phase is indicated in sub-section 1 by double underlining):

Section 2. - Objectives and Powers. The primary objective of the Board is to provide effective and efficient supervision and management of the harbor leasing and developmental activities so as to: (i) maximize the economic benefits inuring to the City by way of the City's contractual and lease arrangements with its harbor lessees; (ii) maximize the public use and patronage of the harbor's recreational facilities and commercial enterprises; (iii) preserve and complement the environmental and aesthetic qualities of the City's harbor and harbor facilities; and (iv) promote and accommodate commerce and navigation in compliance with the Tidelands Grant. In order to accomplish the foregoing objectives, the Board shall:

(1) Review and recommend to the Council on the design and construction of new harbor facilities and related improvements, and any additions to or modifications of such facilities or improvements;

(2) Review and recommend to the Council on the various contractual and lease arrangements relating to the harbor, harbor facilities and related improvements.

Richard W. Parsons
Harbor Director

**HARBOR REVIEW BOARD**

**OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES**

**ARTICLE I: GENERAL**

**Section 1. - Purposes.** These Guidelines are intended to set forth the operational framework within which the Harbor Review Board ("Board") and its Staff shall conduct the Board's business and perform its duties and functions as such matters shall be directed and established by the City Council of the City of Redondo Beach ("Council"). The primary responsibility of the Board is to review and recommend to the City Council on leasing and developmental matters related to the improvement and operation of King Harbor as said harbor is described in Section 12-1.108 of Chapter 1, Title 12 of the Redondo Beach Municipal Code. However, in the performance of these obligations, it is acknowledged that the Board must, to a very substantial degree, depend upon its Staff. Accordingly, a significant portion of these Guidelines is devoted to the specification of matters which are regarded as Staff functions to be handled and dealt with by the Staff without the prior approval of the Board.

**Section 2. - Objectives and Powers.** The primary objective of the Board is to provide effective and efficient supervision and management of the harbor leasing and developmental activities so as to: (i) maximize the economic benefits inuring to the City by way of the City's contractual and lease arrangements with its harbor lessees; (ii) maximize the public use and patronage of the harbor's recreational facilities and commercial enterprises; (iii) preserve and complement the environmental and aesthetic qualities of the City's harbor and harbor facilities; and (iv) promote and accommodate commerce and navigation in compliance with the Tidelands Grant. In order to accomplish the foregoing objectives, the Board shall:

(1) Review and Recommend to the City Council on the design and construction of new harbor facilities and related improvements, and any additions to or modifications of such facilities or improvements;

(2) Review and recommend to the Council on the various contractual and lease arrangements relating to the harbor, harbor facilities and related improvements.

**ARTICLE II: Composition of the Board**

**Section I - General.** The Harbor Review Board shall consist of seven (7) persons selected by the Mayor of Redondo Beach subject to the approval of the City Council of Redondo Beach from among the following:

1.1 two members of the Council;

1.2 Four public members having knowledge or expertise in the following areas:

- recreational harbors and/or commercial real estate;
- architecture and/or planning
- business and/or finance
- building and/or engineering

1.3 one member from among those individuals or entities within the Harbor and Pier areas having a lease or sublease with the City.

Section 2 - Term of Office.

2.1 Members of the Council shall serve on the Board for periods that coincide with their term on the Council;

2.2 Public members shall serve four year terms commencing on July 1st. of any given year. The members first appointed to the Board shall be appointed in such a manner that each succeeding July 1st, the term of one of their number shall expire. Thereafter, any appointment to fill an unexpired term shall be for such unexpired period.

2.3 The lessee member shall serve a one year term commencing on July 1st. of each year.

Section 3 - Organization Meeting. The Board shall meet as soon after the first day of July of every year as practical for the purpose of organizing. At such meeting, the Board shall organize itself by electing a Chairman and establishing a regular monthly meeting date. All proceeding of the Board shall be open to the public.

Section 4 - Vacancies. Vacancies occuring on the Board shall be filled by appointment by the Mayor subject to confirmation by the Council, and in the event that any member of the Board shall absent himself from the regular monthly meeting of the Board for a period of sixty (60) days consecutively following the last regular meeting attended by such member, unless by permission of the Council expressed in its official minutes, or in the event such member shall be convicted of crime involving moral turpitude, his office shall become vacant and shall be so declared by the Council.

ARTICLE III: CONCURRENT POWERS AND FUNCTIONS OF BOARD AND ITS STAFF

Section 1. - General. The Board shall serve as the Council's representative in reviewing leasing and developmental matters pertaining

to the harbor and the pier and, and in so doing, the Board shall act primarily as a recommending body. The Staff will be responsible for implementing the recommendations of the Board and, to the extent authorized by these Guidelines or otherwise directed by the Board, or Council shall be empowered to act and to conduct the Board's business without first obtaining the consent or approval of the Board.

Section 2. - Powers and Functions Vested in the Board. The following powers and functions may be exercised by the Board, except in instances when the Council indicates that a different procedure is to be followed, though such matters will, to a substantial degree, be dependent upon information and data accumulated and recommendations made by the Staff:

(1) The Board shall make reports and recommendations to the Council on all matters relating to leasing and developmental activities within the harbor upon which the Council must act or approve;

(2) All matters within the preview of the Board, which must be submitted to the City Council, shall first be reviewed and/or approved by the Board prior to reporting to the Council.

Section 3. - Board and Staff Responsibilities Pertaining to Lease and Sublease Arrangements.

3.1 Proposals to Lease: The Staff shall serve as the initial contact between the City and potential developers for sites located in the harbor area. Further, the Staff shall screen such proposals based upon information provided to the Staff with respect to the proposed development and the developers and shall select one or more such proposals to be presented to and considered by the Board. The Board shall, after reviewing the Staff's report, take such action as it deems appropriate to arrive at the recommendation to be made to the Council.

3.2 Pre-Lease and Construction Arrangements: Upon the approval of the Council and formalization of the pre-lease and construction arrangement for a proposed project, the Staff shall be responsible for monitoring the developer performance and for reviewing, approving and recommending changes to the conceptual developmental plans, preliminary plans, working drawings, and specifications which may be submitted by the developer in accordance with said contractual arrangement. At any stage of the design process, and only after the Staff is satisfied with the documents and materials submitted, such plans, drawings and specifications may be submitted to the Board for its review, approval and such further actions and recommendations as the Board may deem appropriate.

3.3 Lease or Sublease Arrangements: The Staff shall be responsible for negotiating the terms of the final Lease or Sublease arrangements between the City and a developer/lessee within limits established by the Board, subject to the approval of the Council.

3.4 Administration of Leases and Subleases: The Staff shall monitor the day-to-day operations and activities of the harbor lessees to insure compliance with the terms of their respective lease arrangements with the City. It shall also be the responsibility of the Staff to insure and make the necessary arrangements for compliance by the City with the City's obligations under such lease arrangements. The Staff shall periodically report to the Board as to the results of its activities with respect to these matters.

3.5 Modification Leases and Subleases: The Board shall be responsible for reviewing and approving, and thereafter reporting and recommending to the Council, all matters which may require a formal modification or amendment to a lease between the City and any harbor lessee. The Board shall not have authority to review and approve subleases between the City's lessees and their subtenants.

Section 4. - Delegation of Authority by the Board to the Staff.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Board may delegate to the Staff any or all of its functions pertaining to the lease and pre-lease arrangements above described.

ARTICLE IV: SPECIFIC POWERS AND FUNCTIONS OF STAFF

Section 1. - Operation of Harbor. The Harbor Director and the Staff shall be responsible for the day to day operation of the Harbor and the Board.

Section 2. Ministerial and Minor Discretionary Matters. The Staff may, without Board approval, authorize action and act on matters about which the Board has previously given the Staff objective criteria or measurements or, on other matters which may involve the exercise of personal judgment or discretion, so long as such matters do not have a significant impact upon the harbor or the harbor facilities.

Section 3. - Emergencies. The Staff shall have the power and authority to take such action as may be necessary or appropriate to prevent or mitigate loss of, or damage to, life, health, property, or essential public services in the harbor area which may arise from a sudden unexpected occurrence, which demands immediate action. This authority to act without Board approval shall expire upon the passing or subsidence of such emergency.

Section 4. - Miscellaneous. Without in anyway limiting the foregoing powers and separate functions of the Board, the Staff may act upon the following matters without having first obtained Board approval:

1. Authorizing action in the conduct and performance of normal maintenance, repair and minor alterations to existing harbor facilities by the City or its harbor lessees or their agents;

2. Authorizing the replacement or reconstruction of harbor facilities to their prior or original condition so long as no substantive changes in the use or expansion of such facilities will result from such replacement or reconstruction;

3. Authorizing the construction and/or installation of small structures, improvements, facilities or equipment which will have no significant impact on the harbor, its operation or other facilities, and are in compliance with currently effective lease or other contractual arrangements;

4. Authorizing the installation and/or expansion of utility services and facilities in the harbor area which may be necessary by reason of matters previously approved by the Board or the Council;

5. Authorizing such interim or temporary actions made necessary by reason of matters previously approved by the Board or the Council, or which may arise from an emergency;

6. Authorizing the collection and dissemination of data and information relating to the Board, the harbor or harbor facilities;

7. Authorizing the conduct of planning and feasibility studies as may be directed by the Board or the Council;

8. Authorizing the conduct of inspections of the harbor and harbor facilities pursuant to the duties and functions of the Board, including the establishment of schedules for the conduct of such inspections;

9. Authorizing and/or undertaking other tasks or projects which may be assigned to the Staff by the Board or the Council.

June 25, 1981

Gordon C. Phillips  
City Attorney  
City of Redondo Beach  
415 Diamond Street  
Redondo Beach, CA 90277

Dear Mr. Phillips:

Your letter dated May 20, 1981, was received by the Commission on June 4, 1981. We appreciate your request for written advice with regard to the Harbor Review Board. However, our regulations, 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(b)(1) require that prior to providing written advice:

"The identity of the person whose duties are in question shall be provided with the request for written advice".

Consequently, we must ask that you provide us with the name of the individual on whose behalf you have requested this advice. In addition to seeking our advice, you may wish to request the Attorney General's advice relative to the duties and obligations imposed by Government Code §1090 et seq. Since it is not a part of the Political Reform Act, then we are not authorized to render advice in relation to its provisions.

As soon as we have received this information we will endeavor to promptly respond to your request.
sections.facts:
The Harbor Review Board is a creature of contract, not ordinance. A copy of the lease provision creating the Harbor Review Board and the operational guidelines are enclosed for your examination.

The question arises as to whether or not a Harbor Lessee has a conflict of interest per se under Article I, Section 2(1), (2), and if not, whether such conflict exists as to new development which would have a financial effect on the official's financial interest.

By way of example: The present lessee on the Harbor Review Board maintains and operates a hotel, restaurant, and marina within the harbor. Recent proposals to construct a hotel and parking structure came before the Harbor Review Board. The hotel and

parking structure are in close proximity to the lessee's hotel and marina, and which would effect that lessee's financial interest.

While the Harbor Review Board is designed to render advisory opinions to the council, from time to time, its conclusions have been treated as final on matters considered to be minor in nature.

   o architecture and/or planning;

c) one member from among those individuals or entities within the Harbor and Pier areas having a lease or sublease with the City.

Robert L. Riley, City Manager

By ____________________________
Richard W. Parsons
Harbor Director

Phillips City Attorney City of Redondo Beach 415 Diamond Street Redondo Beach, CA 90277 Dear Mr. Phillips: Your letter dated May 20, 1981, was received by the Commission on June 4, 1981. We appreciate your request for written advice with regard to the Harbor Review Board. However, our regulations, 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329(b)(1) require that prior to providing written advice: "The identity of the person whose duties are in question shall be provided with the request for written advice". Consequently, we must ask that you provide us with the name of the individual on whose behalf you have requested this advice. In addition to seeking our advice, you may wish to request the Attorney General's advice relative to the duties and obligations imposed by Government Code §1090 et seq. Since it is not a part of the Political Reform Act, then we are not authorized to render advice in relation to its provisions. As soon as we have received this information we will endeavor to promptly respond to your request. Sincerely, Robert E. Ledigh Counsel Legal Division REL:gs GORDON C. PHILLIPS CITY ATTORNEY TELEPHONE (213) 372-1171 FPPC Jun 4 8 41 AM '81 CITY OF REDONDO BEACH CALIFORNIA 415 DIAMOND STREET REDONDO BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90277 May 20, 1981 Fair Political Practices Commission P. O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 Gentlemen: QUESTION NO. 1 Does a Harbor Lessee have a conflict of interest per se when sitting as a member of the city's Harbor Review Board? QUESTION NO. 2 If the answer to Question No.1 is "no," does a Harbor Lessee have a conflict of interest in sitting on the Harbor Review Board when making a determination regarding new development within the harbor which has a financial effect on such lessee? FACTUAL BACKGROUND The Harbor Review Board is a creature of contract, not ordinance. A copy of the lease provision creating the Harbor Review Board and the operational guidelines are enclosed for your examination. The question arises as to whether or not a Harbor Lessee has a conflict of interest per se under Article I, Section 2(1), (2), and if not, whether such conflict exists as to new development which would have a financial effect on the official's financial interest. By way of example: The present lessee on the Harbor Review Board maintains and operates a hotel, restaurant, and marina within the harbor. Recent proposals to construct a hotel and parking structure came before the Harbor Review Board. The hotel and Fair Political Practices Commission May 20, 1981 Page 2 parking structure are in close proximity to the lessee's hotel and marina, and which would effect that lessee's financial interest.
